informal_statement.text: Given a sorted slice of integers in nondecreasing order and a target value, return Some(i) where arr[i] == target, or None if the target is absent. If multiple indices match, returning any valid index is acceptable.
preconditions: arr is sorted in nondecreasing order
required_properties: if the function returns Some(i), then i < arr.len() and arr[i] == target
if the function returns None, then target does not occur in arr
the function terminates for every input
edge_cases: single-element slice not containing the target

semantic_units.description: Input precondition: the slice is sorted in nondecreasing order.
common_failure_modes: asserting strict ordering instead of nondecreasing

semantic_units.description: Success postcondition: if the return is Some(i), then i is a valid index and arr[i] == target.
Failure postcondition: if the return is None, then target does not occur in arr.
common_failure_modes: only quantifying over a prefix or suffix
stating the weaker claim that target is not at a specific position

semantic_units.description: Termination: the function terminates on every input (lo..hi shrinks each iteration).
Uniqueness is NOT required: multiple valid indices exist when duplicates are present.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (arr : Arr) (t : Int) (i : Nat), binarySearch arr t = some i → i < arr.length ∧ arr.get? i = some t
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: If the function returns Some i, then i is in range and arr[i] equals the target.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (arr : Arr) (t : Int), SortedLE arr → binarySearch arr t = none → ∀ i, i < arr.length → arr.get? i ≠ some t
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: Assuming the input is sorted in nondecreasing order, if the function returns None then the target does not occur anywhere in the slice.

generated_obligations.nl_gloss: No explicit precondition is asserted by the implementation. The only required property for correctness is that the input slice is sorted nondecreasing, but the code itself does not enforce it.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (arr : Array Int) (target : Int), Sorted (fun i j => arr[i] ≤ arr[j]) (List.ofFn (fun k => arr[k]) ) → True
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Assume the array is sorted in nondecreasing order (i.e., for i < j, arr[i] ≤ arr[j]). This is the standard precondition for binary search correctness.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (arr : Array Int) (target : Int), let n := arr.size; ∀ lo hi : Nat, lo ≤ hi ∧ hi ≤ n → (measure (hi - lo) decreases in each loop iteration)
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: The loop terminates: with lo < hi, each iteration strictly decreases the interval length hi - lo by moving lo up or hi down.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (arr : Array Int) (target : Int), let n := arr.size; ∀ lo hi : Nat, lo ≤ hi ∧ hi ≤ n → (∀ k : Nat, k < lo ∨ k ≥ hi → arr[k] ≠ target)
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Loop invariant: at any point, all indices outside the current search interval [lo, hi) cannot contain the target.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (arr : Array Int) (target : Int), let n := arr.size; ∀ lo hi : Nat, lo ≤ hi ∧ hi ≤ n → (∀ k, k < lo ∨ k ≥ hi → arr[k] ≠ target) → let mid := lo + (hi - lo)/2; (arr[mid] < target → (∀ k, k < (mid+1) ∨ k ≥ hi → arr[k] ≠ target)) ∧ (arr[mid] > target → (∀ k, k < lo ∨ k ≥ mid → arr[k] ≠ target))
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: If the invariant holds before an iteration, it is preserved after updating lo := mid+1 when arr[mid] < target, or hi := mid when arr[mid] > target. (The equality case returns immediately.)

generated_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (arr : Array Int) (target : Int), let n := arr.size; ∀ i : Nat, i < n → (binary_search arr target = some i) → arr[i] = target
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: If the function returns Some(i), then the returned index i is within bounds and the element at that index equals the target.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (arr : Array Int) (target : Int), let n := arr.size; SortedNondecreasing arr → (binary_search arr target = none) → ∀ i : Nat, i < n → arr[i] ≠ target
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: If the function returns None, then the target does not occur anywhere in the array.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (arr : Array Int) (target : Int), SortedNondecreasing arr → (binary_search arr target = some i ↔ ∃ j, j < arr.size ∧ arr[j] = target ∧ i = j) ∨ (binary_search arr target = none ↔ ¬ ∃ j, j < arr.size ∧ arr[j] = target)
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Overall functional specification: for sorted nondecreasing arrays, the result is Some(i) exactly when the target exists (at some index), otherwise None.
